question.text: For an individual with total income of Rs. 2,50,00,000, what is the maximum surcharge rate applicable on long-term capital gains taxed under Section 112 or 112A?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** While normal income surcharge rates can go up to 25% (Rs. 2-5 Cr bracket) or 37% (above Rs. 5 Cr), there is a special cap on surcharge for capital gains. For STCG under Section 111A, LTCG under Sections 112 and 112A, and dividend income, the surcharge is capped at 15% regardless of the total income level. This cap was introduced to prevent excessive taxation on investment income. **The Trap:** 25% is the surcharge rate applicable on normal income in the Rs. 2-5 Cr bracket — but this does NOT apply to capital gains. 37% is the maximum surcharge for normal income above Rs. 5 Cr. 10% is the surcharge for Rs. 50L-1 Cr bracket and applies to normal income. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A and VIII (Equity Derivatives) test surcharge caps on capital gains for portfolio tax planning. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 15% surcharge cap on capital gains is a significant advantage for HNI investors — equity investments become relatively more tax-efficient compared to business income at very high income levels.
